page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r6.xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz. The x86_64 architecture supports AVX-512 instructions, including AVX-512 VNNI for vector neural network workloads. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate that the ecs.r6.xlarge exhibits modest processing capabilities. Stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests place the instance in the bottom 25% tier. Similarly, memory bandwidth tests for both small cached blocks and large uncached blocks fall into the bottom 25% category, though PassMark memory scores reach the middle 50% average tier. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression performs at an average level, whereas multi-threaded bzip3 decompression drops to the bottom 10%. Database operations and Redis SET operations also register in the bottom 25% tier.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation and high memory density, the ecs.r6.xlarge offers a cost-efficient profile for memory-bound workloads that do not require intense computational throughput or local storage. The absence of bundled local storage means storage must be attached externally. This instance is appropriate for memory-intensive applications such as caching layers, small-scale in-memory databases, and general-purpose enterprise applications requiring a larger memory footprint relative to compute resources.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r6.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 32.0 GB of RAM, resulting in an 8.0 GB per core memory allocation. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate modest compute capabilities, with stress-ng CPU and memory bandwidth tests scoring in the bottom 25% tier, though PassMark memory performance reaches the average middle 50% tier. This instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads that require dedicated resources but have moderate CPU demands, such as caching systems, small in-memory databases, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
